frameworks.name: theory-based reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: model-based RL where the internal model is an intuitive causal theory of objects, agents, and goals

frameworks.name: model-based reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: extends model-based RL with structured ontological priors over the world

frameworks.name: alternative learning models
frameworks.one_line: competing computational accounts of value and state representation in PFC

frameworks.name: intuitive theories of physics and psychology
frameworks.one_line: cognitive ontology of objects, agents, relations, and goals as scaffolding

core_question: How does the brain represent and learn the internal models that support planning and generalization in complex environments?
key_assumptions: internal models take the form of structured, causal intuitive theories
theories are built on an ontology of objects, agents, relations, and goals
theory components can be updated separately during learning
manipulations: playing different Atari-style video games during fMRI
modeling brain activity with theory-based vs alternative learning models
tracking events identified as theory-update moments
key_findings: inferred theories explain inferior frontal gyrus and other prefrontal signals better than alternatives
theory-update events evoke increased activity in IFG, occipital, and fusiform cortex
separable learning signals are observed for different theory components
effective connectivity flows top-down from prefrontal theory regions to occipito-temporal update regions
interpretations: prefrontal cortex encodes and dispatches abstract intuitive theories used for planning
theory updating is implemented as top-down signaling that revises perceptual/object representations
